intro: Myrcia piauhiensis, also known as Piauhi, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia piauhiensis include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
